revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[徳島市]]勢見町1丁目1 |位置 = |祭神 = [[大物主|大物主命]] |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[11月10日]] |神事 = |}} '''金刀比羅神社'''（こんぴらじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]麓に位置する。徳島県下三金刀比羅（徳島市[[川内村 (徳島県)|川内町]]宮島、[[鳴門市]]木津）の一つ。
== 歴史 == [[元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）に[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[阿波国]]に入国後、[[徳島城]]府の鎮府として勝占町から移された。近隣にある勢見山は、[[源義経]]が[[屋島]]へ向かうために[[勝浦郡|勝浦]]に上陸し、軍勢を見たことに由来しており、本来は勝占神社の鎮座する鉢伏山のことだが遷座と供に山名も移ってきた。
大鳥居は[[寛政]]3年（[[1791年]]）に築かれ、大鳥居のしめ縄は徳島県下一である。
== 祭神 == * [[大物主|大物主命]]
== 摂社 == * 皇大神社 * 天神社 * 興津神社
== 市中五社 == * [[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]] - 徳島市南佐古町 * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市眉山町大滝山 * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市伊賀町 * 四所神社 - 徳島市福島町
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]「二軒屋・法花」行き「金刀比羅下」下車すぐ。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[二軒屋駅]]より徒歩約13分。
== 関連項目 == * [[金刀比羅神社]] * [[忌部神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002061.html 金刀比羅神社（阿波ナビ）]

== 歴史 == [[元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）に[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[阿波国]]に入国後、[[徳島城]]府の鎮府として勝占町から移された。近隣にある勢見山は、[[源義経]]が[[屋島]]へ向かうために[[勝浦郡|勝浦]]に上陸し、軍勢を見たことに由来しており、本来は勝占神社の鎮座する鉢伏山のことだが遷座と供に山名も移ってきた。

== 摂社 == * 皇大神社 * 天神社 * 興津神社
== 市中五社 == * [[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]] - 徳島市南佐古町 * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市眉山町大滝山 * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市伊賀町 * 四所神社 - 徳島市福島町

== 歴史 == [[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）に[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[阿波国]]に入国後、[[徳島城]]府の鎮府として勝占町から移された。近隣にある勢見山は、[[源義経]]が[[屋島]]へ向かうために[[勝浦郡|勝浦]]に上陸し、軍勢を見たことに由来しており、本来は[[勝占神社]]の鎮座する鉢伏山のことだが遷座と供に山名も移ってきた。
大鳥居は[[寛政]]3年（[[1791年]]）に築かれ、大鳥居のしめ縄は徳島県下一である。
== 祭神 == * [[大物主|大物主命]]
== 摂社 == * 皇大神社 * 天神社 * 興津神社
== 市中五社 == * [[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]] - 徳島市[[南佐古三番町]] * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市[[伊賀町 (徳島市)|伊賀町]] * [[四所神社 (徳島市)|四所神社]] - 徳島市[[福島 (徳島市)|福島]]

{{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kotohira Jinja (Tokushima City).jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[勢見町]]1丁目1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大物主|大物主命]] |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[11月10日]] |神事 = |}} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[勢見町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]麓に位置する。徳島県下三金刀比羅（徳島市[[川内町 (徳島市)|川内町]]宮島、[[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]）の一つ。勢見のこんぴらさんとして親しまれている。
境内には[[日本一の一覧|日本最大]]の大石灯籠（10.24m）があり[[とくしま市民遺産]]に選定されている。
== 歴史 == [[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）に[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[阿波国]]に入国後、[[徳島城]]府の鎮府として[[勝占町]]から移された。近隣にある[[勢見山]]は、[[源義経]]が[[屋島]]へ向かうために[[勝浦郡|勝浦]]に上陸し、軍勢を見たことに由来しており、本来は[[勝占神社]]の鎮座する鉢伏山のことだが遷座と供に山名も移ってきた。
明治18年（[[1885年]]）から同25年（[[1892年]]）まで、[[近代社格制度|国幣中社]]忌部神社が仮鎮座したことがある。なお、その経緯は[[忌部神社]]の項目を参照されたい。
大鳥居は[[寛政]]3年（[[1791年]]）に築かれ、大鳥居のしめ縄は徳島県下一である。

境内には[[日本一の一覧|日本最大]]の大石灯籠（10.24m）があり[[とくしま市民遺産]]に選定されている。
== 歴史 == [[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）に[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[阿波国]]に入国後、[[徳島城]]府の鎮府として[[勝占町]]から移された。近隣にある[[勢見山]]は、[[源義経]]が[[屋島]]へ向かうために[[勝浦郡|勝浦]]に上陸し、軍勢を見たことに由来しており、本来は[[勝占神社]]の鎮座する鉢伏山のことだが遷座と供に山名も移ってきた。
明治18年（[[1885年]]）から同25年（[[1892年]]）まで、[[近代社格制度|国幣中社]]忌部神社が仮鎮座したことがある。なお、その経緯は[[忌部神社]]の項目を参照されたい。
大鳥居は[[寛政]]3年（[[1791年]]）に築かれ、大鳥居のしめ縄は徳島県下一である。また[[川内町 (徳島市)|川内町]]にある[[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|金刀比羅神社]]と[[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]の[[金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|金刀比羅神社]]と合わせて古来より「阿波三金比羅」と呼ばれていた<ref>「徳島県の歴史散歩」（[[2009年]]、[[山川出版社]],p105）</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[大物主|大物主命]]
== 摂社 == * 皇大神社 * 天神社 * 興津神社
== 市中五社 == * [[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]] - 徳島市[[南佐古三番町]] * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市[[伊賀町 (徳島市)|伊賀町]] * [[四所神社 (徳島市)|四所神社]] - 徳島市[[福島 (徳島市)|福島]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[高徳線]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]「二軒屋・法花」行き「金刀比羅下」下車すぐ。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]][[二軒屋駅]]より徒歩約13分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[金刀比羅宮]] * [[金刀比羅神社]] * [[忌部神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002061.html 金刀比羅神社（阿波ナビ）] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや とくしまし}} [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:眉山]]

境内には[[日本一の一覧|日本最大]]の大石灯籠（10.24m）があり[[とくしま市民遺産]]に選定されている。

明治18年（[[1885年]]）から同25年（[[1892年]]）まで、[[近代社格制度|国幣中社]]忌部神社が仮鎮座したことがある。なお、その経緯は[[忌部神社]]の項目を参照されたい。
大鳥居は[[寛政]]3年（[[1791年]]）に築かれ、大鳥居のしめ縄は徳島県下一である。また[[川内町 (徳島市)|川内町]]にある[[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|金刀比羅神社]]と[[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]の[[金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|金刀比羅神社]]と合わせて古来より「阿波三金比羅」と呼ばれていた<ref>「徳島県の歴史散歩」（[[2009年]]、[[山川出版社]],p105）</ref>。

revision.text:
金刀比羅神社_(徳島市)
[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)]] {{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], [[Seimichō|勢見町]] 1-chōme 1 | Coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | AlternateName = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsuga Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}}
== External Links ==

The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsuga Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>

* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine

[[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)]] {{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}} 1-chōme 1 | Coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | AlternateName = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsuga Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

== Related Topics ==

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsuga Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002061.html Kotohira Shrine (Awa Navi)]

Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)]] {{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}} 1-chōme 1 | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsuga Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsuga Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsuga Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Topics ==
* {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}}

[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)]] {{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}} 1-chōme 1 | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsuga Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002061.html Kotohira Shrine (Awa Navi)]

[[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)]] {{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}} 1-chōme 1 | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsuga Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002061.html Kotohira Shrine (Awa Navi)]

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsuga Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002061.html Kotohira Shrine (Awa Navi)]

[[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:U]][[Category:c]][[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 5]][[Category: c]][[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: categories]]

<!--Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)]] {{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}} 1-chōme 1 | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsuga Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002061.html Kotohira Shrine (Awa Navi)]

<!--Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)]] {{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}} 1-chōme 1 | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsuga Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}}
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

<!--Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)]] {{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}} 1-chōme 1 | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

<!--Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)]] {{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}} 1-chōme 1 | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2年}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}== See Also ==
* {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}}
