'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2nd year }} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3年}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.

<!--Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)]] {{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}} 1-chōme 1 | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2nd year }} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3rd year }} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}== See Also ==
* {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002061.html Kotohira Shrine (Awa Navi)]

<!--Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)--> [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)]] {{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}} 1-chōme 1 | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2nd year }} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3rd year }} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also ==

Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市}}. Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3rd year }} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線}}.
== References ==

Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)|12=draft}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|12=draft}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|12=draft}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2nd year|12=draft}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|12=draft}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国|12=draft}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山|12=draft}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=draft}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山|12=draft}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社|12=draft}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|12=draft}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|12=draft}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|12=draft}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3rd year|12=draft}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|12=draft}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>

* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine

The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2nd year|12=draft}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|12=draft}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国|12=draft}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山|12=draft}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=draft}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山|12=draft}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社|12=draft}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|12=draft}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|12=draft}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|12=draft}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3rd year|12=draft}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|12=draft}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町|12=draft}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|12=draft}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線|12=draft}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|12=draft}}.

29 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:金刀比羅神社 (徳島市) up to rev 84208994
revision.text:
{{otheruses|徳島市勢見町にある神社|徳島市川内町にある神社|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)}} {{神社 |名称 = 金刀比羅神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] |所在地 = [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[勢見町]]1丁目1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大物主|大物主命]] |社格 = |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[11月10日]] |神事 = |}} '''金刀比羅神社'''（ことひらじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[勢見町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]麓に位置する。徳島県下三金刀比羅（徳島市[[川内町 (徳島市)|川内町]]宮島、[[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]）の一つ。勢見のこんぴらさんとして親しまれている。
境内には[[日本一の一覧|日本最大]]の大石灯籠（10.24m）があり[[とくしま市民遺産]]に選定されている。
== 歴史 == [[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）に[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[阿波国]]に入国後、[[徳島城]]府の鎮府として[[勝占町]]から移された。近隣にある[[勢見山]]は、[[源義経]]が[[屋島]]へ向かうために[[勝浦郡|勝浦]]に上陸し、軍勢を見たことに由来しており、本来は[[勝占神社]]の鎮座する鉢伏山のことだが遷座と供に山名も移ってきた。
明治18年（[[1885年]]）から同25年（[[1892年]]）まで、[[近代社格制度|国幣中社]]忌部神社が仮鎮座したことがある。なお、その経緯は[[忌部神社]]の項目を参照されたい。
大鳥居は[[寛政]]3年（[[1791年]]）に築かれ、大鳥居のしめ縄は徳島県下一である。また[[川内町 (徳島市)|川内町]]にある[[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|金刀比羅神社]]と[[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]の[[金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|金刀比羅神社]]と合わせて古来より「阿波三金比羅」と呼ばれていた<ref>「徳島県の歴史散歩」（[[2009年]]、[[山川出版社]],p105）</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[大物主|大物主命]]
== 摂社 == * 皇大神社 * 天神社 * 興津神社
== 市中五社 == * [[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]] - 徳島市[[南佐古三番町]] * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市[[伊賀町 (徳島市)|伊賀町]] * [[四所神社 (徳島市)|四所神社]] - 徳島市[[福島 (徳島市)|福島]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[高徳線]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]「二軒屋・法花」行き「金刀比羅下」下車すぐ。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]][[二軒屋駅]]より徒歩約13分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[金刀比羅宮]] * [[金刀比羅神社]] * [[忌部神社]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0002061.html 金刀比羅神社（阿波ナビ）] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ことひらしんしや とくしまし}} [[category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]] [[Category:眉山]]

境内には[[日本一の一覧|日本最大]]の大石灯籠（10.24m）があり[[とくしま市民遺産]]に選定されている。
== 歴史 == [[元和 (日本)|元和]]2年（[[1616年]]）に[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[阿波国]]に入国後、[[徳島城]]府の鎮府として[[勝占町]]から移された。近隣にある[[勢見山]]は、[[源義経]]が[[屋島]]へ向かうために[[勝浦郡|勝浦]]に上陸し、軍勢を見たことに由来しており、本来は[[勝占神社]]の鎮座する鉢伏山のことだが遷座と供に山名も移ってきた。
明治18年（[[1885年]]）から同25年（[[1892年]]）まで、[[近代社格制度|国幣中社]]忌部神社が仮鎮座したことがある。なお、その経緯は[[忌部神社]]の項目を参照されたい。
大鳥居は[[寛政]]3年（[[1791年]]）に築かれ、大鳥居のしめ縄は徳島県下一である。また[[川内町 (徳島市)|川内町]]にある[[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|金刀比羅神社]]と[[鳴門市]][[撫養町木津]]の[[金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|金刀比羅神社]]と合わせて古来より「阿波三金比羅」と呼ばれていた<ref>「徳島県の歴史散歩」（[[2009年]]、[[山川出版社]],p105）</ref>。
== 祭神 == * [[大物主|大物主命]]
== 摂社 == * 皇大神社 * 天神社 * 興津神社
== 市中五社 == * [[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]] - 徳島市[[南佐古三番町]] * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市[[伊賀町 (徳島市)|伊賀町]] * [[四所神社 (徳島市)|四所神社]] - 徳島市[[福島 (徳島市)|福島]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[高徳線]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]「二軒屋・法花」行き「金刀比羅下」下車すぐ。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[牟岐線]][[二軒屋駅]]より徒歩約13分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[金刀比羅宮]] * [[金刀比羅神社]] * [[忌部神社]]

{{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町|12=draft}} 1-chōme 1 | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日|12=draft}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町|12=draft}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)|12=draft}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|12=draft}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|12=draft}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2nd year|12=draft}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|12=draft}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国|12=draft}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山|12=draft}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|12=draft}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=draft}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山|12=draft}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社|12=draft}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|12=draft}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|12=draft}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|12=draft}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3rd year|12=draft}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|12=draft}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主|12=draft}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町|12=draft}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|12=draft}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線|12=draft}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|12=draft}}.

Bizan|qq=}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|qq=}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Heritage|qq=}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1616|qq=}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Iemasa|qq=}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Province|qq=}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Castle|qq=}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimi Mountain|qq=}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|qq=}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibuse Mountain|qq=}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura Shrine|qq=}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhei Chūsha|qq=}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1791|qq=}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|qq=}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Sako Sanban-chō|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizan Town|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iga Town, Tokushima City|qq=}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima, Tokushima City|qq=}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku Line|qq=}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikenya Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi Line|qq=}}.

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}}

Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Bizan|qq=}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|qq=}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Heritage|qq=}}.

The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimi Mountain|qq=}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|qq=}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibuse Mountain|qq=}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura Shrine|qq=}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhei Chūsha|qq=}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1791|qq=}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|qq=}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Sako Sanban-chō|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizan Town|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iga Town, Tokushima City|qq=}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima, Tokushima City|qq=}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku Line|qq=}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikenya Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi Line|qq=}}.

==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture]], [[Tokushima City]], [[Seimicho]] 1-Chome 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Established = | Main hall = | Annual festival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 10|qq=}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (ことひらじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Seimicho]], [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Bizan|qq=}}, it is affectionately known as "Seko's Konpira-san." It is one of the three major Kotohira Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchi Town, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawauchi Town, Tokushima City|qq=}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}}, specifically in {{ill|Kizucho, Naruto City|ja|撫養町木津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizucho, Naruto City|qq=}}.
The shrine grounds feature the largest stone lantern in Japan, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been selected as a {{ill|Tokushima Civic Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Civic Heritage|qq=}}.
== History == The shrine was moved to its current location in [[1616]] (Genwa 2) after {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Iemasa|qq=}} entered [[Awa Province]] and established [[Tokushima Castle]]. It was originally relocated from {{ill|Kachiden Town|ja|勝占町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kachiden Town|qq=}} as the tutelary shrine for the castle town. The nearby {{ill|Mt. Seimi|ja|勢見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Seimi|qq=}} derives its name from a legend in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} surveyed his troops en route to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|qq=}} after landing in Katsuura.
From [[1885]] (Meiji 18) to [[1892]] (Meiji 25), the shrine temporarily housed the {{ill|Imibe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe Shrine|qq=}}, which was designated a {{ill|National Middle Shrine|ja|国幣中社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Middle Shrine|qq=}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]]. For more details, refer to the article on {{ill|Imibe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe Shrine|qq=}}.
The grand torii gate was constructed in [[1791]] (Kansei 3), and the shrine's shimenawa (sacred rope) is the largest in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the Kotohira Shrines in Kawauchi and Naruto, the shrine has been historically referred to as one of the "Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa."<ref>"Tokushima History Walk" (2009, Yamakawa Publishing, p.105)</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-shrines == * Kōtai Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Okitsu Shrine
== City's Five Shrines == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki, [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * From [[JR]] [[Kōtoku Line]]'s [[Tokushima Station]], take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikoya/Hokka" and get off at "Kotohira Shita." The shrine is a short walk from the stop. * Approximately 13 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Mugi Line]]'s [[Nikoya Station]].
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira-gū|qq=}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}}

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimichō|qq=}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Bizan|qq=}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|qq=}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Heritage|qq=}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1616|qq=}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Iemasa|qq=}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Province|qq=}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Castle|qq=}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimi Mountain|qq=}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|qq=}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibuse Mountain|qq=}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura Shrine|qq=}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhei Chūsha|qq=}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1791|qq=}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|qq=}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Sako Sanban-chō|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizan Town|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iga Town, Tokushima City|qq=}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima, Tokushima City|qq=}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku Line|qq=}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikenya Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi Line|qq=}}.

==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture]], [[Tokushima City]], [[Seimicho]] 1-Chome 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Established = | Main hall = | Annual festival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 10|qq=}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (ことひらじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Seimicho]], [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Bizan|qq=}}, it is affectionately known as "Seko's Konpira-san." It is one of the three major Kotohira Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchi Town, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawauchi Town, Tokushima City|qq=}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}}, specifically in {{ill|Kizucho, Naruto City|ja|撫養町木津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizucho, Naruto City|qq=}}.
The shrine grounds feature the largest stone lantern in Japan, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been selected as a {{ill|Tokushima Civic Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Civic Heritage|qq=}}.
== History == The shrine was moved to its current location in [[1616]] (Genwa 2) after {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Iemasa|qq=}} entered [[Awa Province]] and established [[Tokushima Castle]]. It was originally relocated from {{ill|Kachiden Town|ja|勝占町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kachiden Town|qq=}} as the tutelary shrine for the castle town. The nearby {{ill|Mt. Seimi|ja|勢見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Seimi|qq=}} derives its name from a legend in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} surveyed his troops en route to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|qq=}} after landing in Katsuura.
From [[1885]] (Meiji 18) to [[1892]] (Meiji 25), the shrine temporarily housed the {{ill|Imibe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe Shrine|qq=}}, which was designated a {{ill|National Middle Shrine|ja|国幣中社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Middle Shrine|qq=}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]]. For more details, refer to the article on {{ill|Imibe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe Shrine|qq=}}.
The grand torii gate was constructed in [[1791]] (Kansei 3), and the shrine's shimenawa (sacred rope) is the largest in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the Kotohira Shrines in Kawauchi and Naruto, the shrine has been historically referred to as one of the "Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa."<ref>"Tokushima History Walk" (2009, Yamakawa Publishing, p.105)</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-shrines == * Kōtai Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Okitsu Shrine
== City's Five Shrines == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki, [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * From [[JR]] [[Kōtoku Line]]'s [[Tokushima Station]], take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikoya/Hokka" and get off at "Kotohira Shita." The shrine is a short walk from the stop. * Approximately 13 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Mugi Line]]'s [[Nikoya Station]].

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimichō|qq=}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Bizan|qq=}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|qq=}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Heritage|qq=}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1616|qq=}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Iemasa|qq=}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Province|qq=}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Castle|qq=}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimi Mountain|qq=}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|qq=}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibuse Mountain|qq=}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura Shrine|qq=}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhei Chūsha|qq=}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1791|qq=}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|qq=}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>

== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Sako Sanban-chō|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizan Town|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iga Town, Tokushima City|qq=}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima, Tokushima City|qq=}}

* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku Line|qq=}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikenya Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi Line|qq=}}.

==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture]], [[Tokushima City]], [[Seimicho]] 1-Chome 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Established = | Main hall = | Annual festival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 10|qq=}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (ことひらじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Seimicho]], [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Bizan|qq=}}, it is affectionately known as "Seko's Konpira-san." It is one of the three major Kotohira Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchi Town, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawauchi Town, Tokushima City|qq=}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}}, specifically in {{ill|Kizucho, Naruto City|ja|撫養町木津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizucho, Naruto City|qq=}}.
The shrine grounds feature the largest stone lantern in Japan, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been selected as a {{ill|Tokushima Civic Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Civic Heritage|qq=}}.
== History == The shrine was moved to its current location in [[1616]] (Genwa 2) after {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Iemasa|qq=}} entered [[Awa Province]] and established [[Tokushima Castle]]. It was originally relocated from {{ill|Kachiden Town|ja|勝占町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kachiden Town|qq=}} as the tutelary shrine for the castle town. The nearby {{ill|Mt. Seimi|ja|勢見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Seimi|qq=}} derives its name from a legend in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} surveyed his troops en route to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|qq=}} after landing in Katsuura.
From [[1885]] (Meiji 18) to [[1892]] (Meiji 25), the shrine temporarily housed the {{ill|Imibe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe Shrine|qq=}}, which was designated a {{ill|National Middle Shrine|ja|国幣中社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Middle Shrine|qq=}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]]. For more details, refer to the article on {{ill|Imibe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe Shrine|qq=}}.
The grand torii gate was constructed in [[1791]] (Kansei 3), and the shrine's shimenawa (sacred rope) is the largest in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the Kotohira Shrines in Kawauchi and Naruto, the shrine has been historically referred to as one of the "Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa."<ref>"Tokushima History Walk" (2009, Yamakawa Publishing, p.105)</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-shrines == * Kōtai Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Okitsu Shrine
== City's Five Shrines == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki, [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * From [[JR]] [[Kōtoku Line]]'s [[Tokushima Station]], take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikoya/Hokka" and get off at "Kotohira Shita." The shrine is a short walk from the stop. * Approximately 13 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Mugi Line]]'s [[Nikoya Station]].
== Notes ==

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimichō|qq=}} 1-chōme 1 | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 10|qq=}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimichō|qq=}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Bizan|qq=}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|qq=}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Heritage|qq=}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1616|qq=}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Iemasa|qq=}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Province|qq=}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Castle|qq=}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimi Mountain|qq=}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|qq=}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibuse Mountain|qq=}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura Shrine|qq=}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhei Chūsha|qq=}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1791|qq=}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|qq=}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Sako Sanban-chō|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizan Town|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iga Town, Tokushima City|qq=}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima, Tokushima City|qq=}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku Line|qq=}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikenya Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi Line|qq=}}.
== References ==

== See Also ==

{{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimichō|qq=}} 1-chōme 1 | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 10|qq=}} | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimichō|qq=}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Bizan|qq=}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|qq=}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Heritage|qq=}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1616|qq=}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Iemasa|qq=}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Province|qq=}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Castle|qq=}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimi Mountain|qq=}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|qq=}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibuse Mountain|qq=}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura Shrine|qq=}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhei Chūsha|qq=}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1791|qq=}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|qq=}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Sako Sanban-chō|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizan Town|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iga Town, Tokushima City|qq=}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima, Tokushima City|qq=}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku Line|qq=}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikenya Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi Line|qq=}}.

==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg|300px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture]], [[Tokushima City]], [[Seimicho]] 1-Chome 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|3|38.85|N|134|32|46.68|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Established = | Main hall = | Annual festival = {{ill|November 10|ja|11月10日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 10|qq=}} }} '''Kotohira Shrine''' (ことひらじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Seimicho]], [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Bizan|qq=}}, it is affectionately known as "Seko's Konpira-san." It is one of the three major Kotohira Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchi Town, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawauchi Town, Tokushima City|qq=}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}}, specifically in {{ill|Kizucho, Naruto City|ja|撫養町木津|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizucho, Naruto City|qq=}}.
The shrine grounds feature the largest stone lantern in Japan, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been selected as a {{ill|Tokushima Civic Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Civic Heritage|qq=}}.
== History == The shrine was moved to its current location in [[1616]] (Genwa 2) after {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Iemasa|qq=}} entered [[Awa Province]] and established [[Tokushima Castle]]. It was originally relocated from {{ill|Kachiden Town|ja|勝占町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kachiden Town|qq=}} as the tutelary shrine for the castle town. The nearby {{ill|Mt. Seimi|ja|勢見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Seimi|qq=}} derives its name from a legend in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} surveyed his troops en route to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|qq=}} after landing in Katsuura.
From [[1885]] (Meiji 18) to [[1892]] (Meiji 25), the shrine temporarily housed the {{ill|Imibe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe Shrine|qq=}}, which was designated a {{ill|National Middle Shrine|ja|国幣中社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Middle Shrine|qq=}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]]. For more details, refer to the article on {{ill|Imibe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe Shrine|qq=}}.
The grand torii gate was constructed in [[1791]] (Kansei 3), and the shrine's shimenawa (sacred rope) is the largest in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the Kotohira Shrines in Kawauchi and Naruto, the shrine has been historically referred to as one of the "Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa."<ref>"Tokushima History Walk" (2009, Yamakawa Publishing, p.105)</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-shrines == * Kōtai Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Okitsu Shrine
== City's Five Shrines == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki, [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * From [[JR]] [[Kōtoku Line]]'s [[Tokushima Station]], take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikoya/Hokka" and get off at "Kotohira Shita." The shrine is a short walk from the stop. * Approximately 13 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Mugi Line]]'s [[Nikoya Station]].

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimichō|qq=}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Bizan|qq=}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|qq=}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|qq=}} and {{ill|Naruto City|ja|鳴門市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruto City|qq=}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Heritage|qq=}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|ja|元和2nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1616|qq=}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Iemasa|qq=}} entered {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Province|qq=}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Castle|qq=}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimi Mountain|qq=}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|qq=}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibuse Mountain|qq=}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura Shrine|qq=}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhei Chūsha|qq=}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|ja|寛政3rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1791|qq=}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|qq=}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi no Mikoto|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi no Mikoto|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Sako Sanban-chō|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizan Town|qq=}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iga Town, Tokushima City|qq=}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima, Tokushima City|qq=}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|ja|徳島駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|ja|高徳線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtoku Line|qq=}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nikenya Station|ja|二軒屋駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikenya Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|ja|牟岐線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi Line|qq=}}.

== History == The shrine was moved to its current location in [[1616]] (Genwa 2) after {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|ja|蜂須賀家政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Iemasa|qq=}} entered [[Awa Province]] and established [[Tokushima Castle]]. It was originally relocated from {{ill|Kachiden Town|ja|勝占町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kachiden Town|qq=}} as the tutelary shrine for the castle town. The nearby {{ill|Mt. Seimi|ja|勢見山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Seimi|qq=}} derives its name from a legend in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|ja|源義経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qq=}} surveyed his troops en route to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|qq=}} after landing in Katsuura.
From [[1885]] (Meiji 18) to [[1892]] (Meiji 25), the shrine temporarily housed the {{ill|Imibe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe Shrine|qq=}}, which was designated a {{ill|National Middle Shrine|ja|国幣中社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Middle Shrine|qq=}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]]. For more details, refer to the article on {{ill|Imibe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe Shrine|qq=}}.
The grand torii gate was constructed in [[1791]] (Kansei 3), and the shrine's shimenawa (sacred rope) is the largest in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the Kotohira Shrines in Kawauchi and Naruto, the shrine has been historically referred to as one of the "Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa."<ref>"Tokushima History Walk" (2009, Yamakawa Publishing, p.105)</ref>
== deity == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-shrines == * Kōtai Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Okitsu Shrine
== City's Five Shrines == * {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki, [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * From [[JR]] [[Kōtoku Line]]'s [[Tokushima Station]], take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikoya/Hokka" and get off at "Kotohira Shita." The shrine is a short walk from the stop. * Approximately 13 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Mugi Line]]'s [[Nikoya Station]].
