{{Otheruses|a shrine located in Seimichō, Tokushima City|a shrine located in Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō, Tokushima City)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kotohirajinja-seimicho.jpeg | location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|qid=Q160734|lt=Tokushima|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}, [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima]], {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimichō|1=Seimicho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400568}} 1-chōme 1 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.0607917|N|134.5463|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) | Classification = | Established = | MainHall = | Alternatename = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|November 10|en|November 10|qid=Q2988|lt=November 10|1=November 10}} | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Tokushima|qid=Q207309|1=Tokushima (city)}} | deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located in {{ill|Seimichō|ja|勢見町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimichō|1=Seimicho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400568}}, Tokushima City, Tokushima Prefecture. Situated at the base of {{ill|Mount Bizan|en|Mount Bizan|qid=Q8189109|lt=Mt. Bizan|1=Mount Bizan}}, it is one of the three significant {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrines|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrines|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Tokushima Prefecture, alongside those in {{ill|Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|ja|川内町 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawauchichō, Tokushima City|1=Kawauchichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11477745}} and {{ill|Naruto, Tokushima|en|Naruto, Tokushima|qid=Q460812|lt=Naruto City|1=Naruto, Tokushima}}. Locals affectionately refer to it as the "Kotohira of Seimi."
The shrine grounds house Japan’s largest stone lantern, standing 10.24 meters tall, which has been designated a {{ill|Tokushima City Heritage|ja|とくしま市民遺産|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima City Heritage|1=Q11273094|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11273094}}.
== History ==
The shrine was relocated in {{ill|1616|en|1616|qid=Q6788|lt=1616|1=1616}} (Genna 2) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|en|Hachisuka Iemasa|qid=Q1073557|lt=Hachisuka Iemasa|1=Hachisuka Iemasa}} entered {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|en|Awa Province (Tokushima)|qid=Q907457|lt=Awa Province|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} and established it as a tutelary shrine for {{ill|Tokushima Castle|en|Tokushima Castle|qid=Q8011246|lt=Tokushima Castle|1=Tokushima Castle}}. The nearby {{ill|Seimi Mountain|ja|勢見山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimi Mountain|1=seimiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400566}} derives its name from an incident in which {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|qid=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}} surveyed enemy forces on his way to {{ill|Yashima|ja|屋島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashima|1=Yashima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11465681}}. Originally, the mountain referred to {{ill|Hachibuse Mountain|ja|鉢伏山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibuse Mountain|1=hachibuseyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11649837}}, where {{ill|Katsura Shrine|ja|勝占神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsura Shrine|qq=}} resides, but the name migrated with the shrine's relocation.
Between 1885 (Meiji 18) and 1892 (Meiji 25), the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|en|Inbe Shrine|qid=Q11490722|lt=Inbe Shrine|1=Inbe Shrine}} was temporarily enshrined here as a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhei Chūsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Details of this period can be found in the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|en|Inbe Shrine|qid=Q11490722|lt=Inbe Shrine|1=Inbe Shrine}} entry.
The grand torii was built in {{ill|1791|en|1791|qid=Q7784|lt=1791|1=1791}} (Kansei 3), featuring the largest shimenawa in Tokushima Prefecture. Together with the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市川内町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kawauchichō)|qq=}} and the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (鳴門市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Naruto)|qq=}}, it is one of the “Awa San Konpira” (Three Kotohira Shrines of Awa).<ref>''History Walks of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2009, Yamakawa Shuppansha, p. 105)</ref>
== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} ([[Konpira Gongen]])
== Sub-Shrines ==
* Kōdaijin Shrine * Tenjin Shrine * Ōkitsu Shrine
== Five Shrines of Tokushima City ==
* {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja|南佐古三番町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami Sako Sanban-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南佐古|1=Minamisako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11407479}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizan Town|1=Bizanchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11582582}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iga Town, Tokushima City|1=Igachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11380671}} * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} – located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima, Tokushima City|1=Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11592983}}
== Access ==
* By bus: Take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikenya・Hōge" from {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|qid=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station|1=Tokushima Station}} on the {{ill|Kōtoku Line|en|Kōtoku Line|qid=Q859984|lt=Kōtoku Line|1=Kōtoku Line}} and alight at "Kotohira Shita," immediately outside the shrine. * By train: Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|Niken'ya Station|en|Niken'ya Station|qid=Q10880415|lt=Nikenya Station|1=Niken'ya Station}} on the {{ill|Mugi Line|en|Mugi Line|qid=Q860478|lt=Mugi Line|1=Mugi Line}}.

Ōtaki, [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * From {{ill|JR|en|JR|qid=Q127942|lt=JR|1=JR}} {{ill|Kōtoku Line|en|Kōtoku Line|qid=Q859984|lt=Kōtoku Line|1=Kōtoku Line}}'s {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|qid=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station|1=Tokushima Station}}, take the Tokushima City Bus bound for "Nikoya/Hokka" and get off at "Kotohira Shita." The shrine is a short walk from the stop. * Approximately 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|JR|en|JR|qid=Q127942|lt=JR|1=JR}} {{ill|Mugi Line|en|Mugi Line|qid=Q860478|lt=Mugi Line|1=Mugi Line}}'s [[Nikoya Station]].
